title: 10 best herbal tinctures for bleeding gums
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for bleeding gums are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, prepared by soaking the plant material in alcohol or vinegar. These tinctures can help alleviate bleeding gums by reducing inflammation and promoting gum tissue health. Examples of herbal tinctures that can be beneficial for bleeding gums include goldenseal, echinacea, and myrrh. Goldenseal has antimicrobial properties that can help control gum infections, while echinacea boosts the immune system to speed up healing. Myrrh has astringent properties that can tighten gum tissue and reduce bleeding. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can experience improved oral health, reduced pain and discomfort, and enhanced quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and styptic properties that can help reduce inflammation and stop bleeding. The herb's astringent properties can help tighten and strengthen the gums, reducing the likelihood of gum bleeding. Additionally, yarrow has been shown to have antibacterial and antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate the bacteria that cause gum disease and lead to bleeding gums. By promoting gum health and reducing inflammation, yarrow tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of bleeding gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from bleeding. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tincture help tighten and strengthen gum tissues, reducing the likelihood of bleeding. This is attributed to tannins found in yarrow.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial properties help fight harmful bacteria in the mouth, promoting oral health and reducing gum inflammation. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, such as azulene and camphor.
Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with bleeding gums. This is attributed to alkaloids and sesquiterpene lactones found in yarrow.
Wound healing: Yarrow tincture promotes wound healing, helping to repair damaged gum tissue. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and polyphenolic acids, which stimulate tissue regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, or use 1 ounce of dried yarrow, and chop finely.
Combine herbs with 1 pint of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out excess liquid.
Store the finished yarrow tincture in a dark glass bottle and use 10-15 drops up to 3 times daily for bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lady's mantle tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten and constrict tissues, reducing excess bleeding. Its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects also help soothe irritation and promote oral health by reducing inflammation and fighting harmful bacteria that can cause gum disease. Additionally, lady's mantle tincture is rich in flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds that support collagen formation and tissue repair, contributing to the healing process of damaged gums. Overall, lady's mantle tincture offers a natural and effective way to alleviate bleeding gums and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Lady's mantle tincture has an astringent property that tightens and strengthens the gum tissue, reducing bleeding. This is due to its tannin content.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory property reduces inflammation in the gums, alleviating pain and swelling, thanks to its salicylic acid and flavonoid content.
Antimicrobial: Lady's mantle tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, promoting oral health and reducing gum inflammation, due to its phenolic acid content.
Wound healing: Its wound healing property promotes the repair of damaged gum tissue, reducing bleeding and fostering recovery, attributed to its tannin and flavonoid content.

Antioxidant: Lady's mantle tincture has antioxidant properties that protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall oral health, due to its phenolic acid and flavonoid content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 handfuls of fresh lady's mantle leaves and flowers, ideally on a sunny morning after the dew has evaporated.
Coarsely chop the herbs and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters full.
Pour 80-proof vodka or rum into the jar, ensuring the herbs are completely submerged, and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, gently shaking it every few days.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the spent herbs, and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) of the tincture up to three times a day for bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's astringent properties can also help to tighten and strengthen gum tissue, reducing the likelihood of bleeding. Additionally, pot marigold is rich in vitamin C and other antioxidants that can support collagen production and promote healing in the gums. By using pot marigold tincture regularly, individuals may be able to improve their gum health, reduce bleeding, and prevent further damage to their teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in the gums, providing relief from bleeding and swelling, due to the presence of flavonoids such as lutein and zeaxanthin.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, reducing the risk of gum infections and bleeding, thanks to the active constituents like essential oils and alkaloids.
Astringent: Pot marigold tincture has astringent properties that help tighten and strengthen gum tissues, reducing bleeding and promoting healing, due to the tannins present in the plant.

Wound healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is crucial for treating bleeding gums and fostering healthy gum tissue growth, attributed to the plant's triterpenoid content.
Analgesic: Pot marigold tincture provides pain relief, alleviating discomfort caused by bleeding gums, as a result of its various analgesic constituents, such as salicylic acid and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, gently rinse, and let dry for 12 hours.
Chop dried flowers into small pieces, filling a clean jar halfway with herb, then add 80-proof vodka until flowers are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly, store in a cool, dark place for two weeks, shaking daily.
After two weeks, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the preparation date and store in a cool, dark place for up to one year, using 10-15 drops as needed for bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria in the mouth. This herb is also rich in vitamin C and other nutrients that are essential for maintaining healthy gums and connective tissues. By improving circulation and strengthening the capillaries in the gums, gotu kola tincture can help reduce bleeding, swelling, and pain associated with gum disease. Its astringent properties can also help tighten and tone the gum tissue, providing additional protection against infection and further damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tincture helps reduce inflammation in the gums, which is important for bleeding gums as inflammation can worsen bleeding and lead to gum damage; the active constituents responsible for this property include triterpenoid saponins, such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Wound healing: Gotu kola tincture promotes wound healing in the gums, which is crucial for bleeding gums as it aids in tissue regeneration and reduces bleeding; the active constituents responsible for this property include triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which stimulate collagen synthesis and improve blood flow.

Antimicrobial: Gotu kola tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps prevent and treat gum infections; the active constituents responsible for this property include pentacyclic triterpenoid acids, such as asiatic, madecassic, and maslinic acids.

Antioxidant: Gotu kola tincture neutralizes free radicals in the gums, which is crucial for bleeding gums as it helps protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and inflammation; the active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as quercetin, luteolin, and apigenin.

Anti-coagulant: Gotu kola tincture reduces blood clotting, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps improve circulation and reduce excessive bleeding; the active constituents responsible for this property include triterpene glycosides, such as brahmoside and brahminoside.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried gotu kola herb, place in a clean jar, then add 1 pint of vodka or rum (80-100 proof).
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from light and heat.
For bleeding gums, use 2-3 drops of the gotu kola tincture on the affected area, 2-3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. Sage is also rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten and strengthen gum tissue, reducing the likelihood of bleeding. Additionally, sage has been shown to help improve blood circulation, which can promote healing and tissue regeneration in the gums. When used regularly, sage tincture can help maintain healthy gums and prevent the development of gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gum inflammation and swelling, providing relief for bleeding gums. This is due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid.

Antimicrobial: Sage tincture's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, reducing the risk of gum infections and bleeding. The active constituents responsible for this property include camphor, camphene, and 1,8-cineole.

Astringent: Sage tincture's astringent properties help tighten and strengthen gum tissues, reducing their tendency to bleed. This is due to the tannins present in sage, which have a contracting effect on mucous membranes.

Analgesic: Sage tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with bleeding gums. The active constituents responsible for this property include α-thujone and β-thujone.

Antioxidant: Sage tincture's antioxidant properties help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall gum health. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sage leaves, filling a clean, dry jar halfway, ensuring they're completely dry to prevent mold growth.
Pour 100 proof alcohol (or a 50/50 mixture of alcohol and distilled water) into the jar, covering the sage leaves by 1-2 inches, releasing air bubbles by gently tapping the jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day to ensure even extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the liquid from the sage leaves.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and recommended dosage (e.g., 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, for bleeding gums), and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains allantoin, a compound known for its ability to promote cell growth and speed up the healing process. This makes it effective in treating gum injuries and reducing inflammation, which can help stop bleeding. Additionally, comfrey has astringent properties that can tighten tissues, reducing the flow of blood to the affected area. Its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects can also provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with bleeding gums. Regular use of comfrey tincture can improve gum health, reduce the risk of gum disease, and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid, helps reduce swelling and irritation in the gums, alleviating bleeding.
Astringent: The astringent property of comfrey tincture, attributed to tannins, helps constrict blood vessels and tighten gum tissues, thereby controlling bleeding and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial: Comfrey tincture's antimicrobial property, owing to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids, helps eliminate harmful bacteria in the mouth, preventing gum infections that can cause bleeding.

Wound healing: Comfrey tincture's wound healing property, attributed to allantoin and other constituents, promotes the growth of new tissue, helping to repair damaged gums and prevent further bleeding.

Pain relief: The pain-relieving property of comfrey tincture, due to the presence of various alkaloids and rosmarinic acid, helps alleviate discomfort caused by bleeding gums, providing quick and effective relief.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried comfrey root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the comfrey root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid from the jar, discarding the solids, and store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle.
Use 5-10 drops of the comfrey tincture on bleeding gums, up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains a range of beneficial compounds, including isoflavones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and blood clotting properties. These properties can help to reduce inflammation in the gums, strengthen blood vessels, and promote tissue repair, all of which can help to alleviate bleeding and improve overall gum health. Additionally, red clover has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce the levels of harmful bacteria in the mouth that can contribute to gum disease and bleeding. By promoting gum health and reducing inflammation, red clover tincture can help to prevent and treat bleeding gums, leading to improved oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Red clover tincture helps reduce inflammation in the gums, which is important for bleeding gums as inflammation can worsen the condition and lead to infection. The anti-inflammatory properties come from isoflavones, such as genistein and daidzein.

Antimicrobial: Red clover tincture has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, which can cause gum inflammation and bleeding. The antimicrobial activity is attributed to the isoflavones and essential oils found in red clover.

Anticoagulant: Red clover tincture can help prevent excessive blood clotting, which is beneficial for bleeding gums as it promotes proper blood flow and healing. The anticoagulant properties are due to the presence of coumarins, such as coumarin and its derivatives.

Wound healing: Red clover tincture promotes wound healing, which is crucial for bleeding gums as it aids in the repair of damaged gum tissue. The wound healing properties are attributed to the isoflavones, which have been shown to stimulate tissue growth and regeneration.

Analgesic: Red clover tincture has pain-relieving properties that help alleviate discomfort associated with bleeding gums. The analgesic effects are due to the presence of various compounds, such as isoflavones, essential oils, and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried red clover flowers and leaves, placing them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Secure a lid on the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the plant material.
Store the red clover tincture in a dark glass bottle or jar, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) three times a day for bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb is rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten and strengthen gum tissue, reducing the likelihood of bleeding. Additionally, plantain tincture contains vitamin A, which is essential for maintaining healthy mucous membranes in the mouth and promoting wound healing. By using plantain tincture regularly, individuals can help improve their oral health and reduce the symptoms of bleeding gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation in the gums, which is important for bleeding gums as inflammation can worsen the condition and lead to periodontal disease. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin.

Astringent: Plantain tincture has astringent properties that help tighten and constrict tissues, reducing bleeding and promoting healing. Tannins, such as catechins and gallic acid, are the primary active constituents responsible for this property.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation and bleeding. The active constituents responsible for this property include allantoin, aucubin, and other phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: Plantain tincture contains antioxidants that help protect gum tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bleeding gums. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids.

Wound healing: Plantain tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps to repair damaged tissues and prevent further bleeding. The active constituents responsible for this property include allantoin, aucubin, and various polysaccharides.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves from a clean, pesticide-free source, choosing young, healthy leaves for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly, then chop or blend them finely to increase the surface area for extraction.
Fill a clean glass jar with the chopped plantain leaves, then pour 100-proof vodka over the leaves until fully submerged, covering the jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days to ensure even extraction.

After the extraction period, strain the liquid from the jar using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid from the leaves, and store the resulting herbal tincture in a dark glass bottle for up to a year. Use 1-2 dropperfuls of the tincture as needed for bleeding gums, swishing it around the mouth before swallowing or spitting it out.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties can help reduce the inflammation in the gums that can lead to bleeding, as well as fight off the bacteria that can cause gum disease and other oral health issues. Additionally, licorice has been shown to help stimulate the production of collagen, a protein that is essential for maintaining the health and structure of the gums. This can help to strengthen the gums and reduce the likelihood of bleeding. Overall, licorice tincture can be a useful natural remedy for promoting oral health and preventing bleeding gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gum swelling and pain, which is important for bleeding gums as inflammation is often the cause of the bleeding. This is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help combat harmful bacteria in the mouth, contributing to improved gum health and reduced bleeding. The active constituents responsible are glycyrrhizic acid, flavonoids, and coumarins.
Wound-healing: Licorice tincture promotes wound healing, which is crucial for bleeding gums as it helps to quickly repair damaged tissue. The active constituent responsible is glycyrrhizin.

Immune-boosting: The immune-boosting properties of licorice tincture support the body's ability to fight infections, which can help prevent or treat gum diseases causing bleeding. The active constituents responsible are glycyrrhizin and triterpene saponins.

Antioxidant: Licorice tincture's antioxidant properties protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage, which is important for maintaining gum health and reducing bleeding. The active constituents responsible are flavonoids and coumarins.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the licorice root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and use 1-2 dropperfuls daily for bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with bleeding gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce swelling and tighten tissues in the mouth. Its tannins can help to constrict blood vessels, which can slow down bleeding and promote healing. Additionally, witch hazel has antimicrobial properties that can help to fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth, contributing to overall oral health and reducing the risk of gum disease. By using witch hazel tincture as a mouthwash or compress, individuals can experience relief from bleeding gums and support their oral health naturally.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Witch hazel tincture is a potent astringent, which helps to tighten and strengthen blood vessels and gum tissue, reducing bleeding and promoting healing. This property is due to the high tannin content, particularly gallotannins and protocatechic acid.

Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from pain and irritation. The primary anti-inflammatory constituents include gallic acid, tannins, and essential oils such as eugenol and hexenol.

Antimicrobial: Witch hazel tincture exhibits antimicrobial effects that help control bacterial growth in the mouth, contributing to healthier gums and fresher breath. The antimicrobial properties can be attributed to the presence of volatile oils, flavonoids, and tannins.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel contains potent antioxidants that protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, supporting overall oral health. The antioxidant activity is primarily due to the high levels of flavonoids, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and myricetin.

Healing and regenerative: Witch hazel promotes tissue repair and regeneration, helping to heal damaged gums and restore their natural function. This healing property is attributed to the tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids present in witch hazel, which stimulate collagen synthesis and cell proliferation.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried witch hazel bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the witch hazel bark, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solid plant material.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and any warnings, and store in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bleeding gums with herbal tinctures is by using a tincture of calendula and myrrh. Calendula has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling and bleeding, while myrrh has antimicrobial properties that can help fight gum infections. Mix equal parts of calendula and myrrh tinctures, then dilute the mixture with an equal amount of water. Swish the solution in your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out. Repeat this process two to three times a day, especially after brushing and flossing. However, it's important to note that while herbal tinctures can help alleviate symptoms, they should not replace regular dental check-ups and professional cleaning. If bleeding gums persist, consult your dentist or healthcare provider.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bleeding gums that are treated with herbal tinctures include gingivitis and periodontitis. Gingivitis is inflammation of the gums, while periodontitis is a severe form of gum disease that can lead to tooth loss. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from goldenseal, calendula, and myrrh, can help to reduce inflammation, fight infection, and promote healing in the gums. These herbs have antibacterial, antiviral, and astringent properties that can help to stop bleeding, reduce pain, and improve overall gum health. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal tinctures to treat any medical condition.
